mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces S&P 500 earnings down and drives negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST rise together, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns in early-cycle periods, with a mean return difference indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 220.0 indicates accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the negative correlation breaks down during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative or flat 60-day SPX returns in more than half of observed instances, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit growth, which translates into immediate positive market repricing and S&P 500 gains over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day SPX forward returns across multiple quarters, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below 90.0 signals a contraction in manufacturing capacity and supply chain stress, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of instances when INDPRO crosses below 90.0, or the signal fails to predict downturns during inventory adjustment phases.
